text.title: Reliability Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Reliability Architecture` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every service ships with an SLO + matching error budget. SLO without budget policy = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Error budget is what makes the SLO actionable.

rule.statement: Service-to-service calls MUST be wrapped in circuit-breaker + timeout + bulkhead + retry-with-jitter. Bare HTTP clients = REJECT.
Service declares ≥ 2 degradation tiers (e.g. full / cached / read-only) with measurable triggers.
rule.rationale: All-or-nothing services lose more revenue under partial failure.

rule.statement: Health-check endpoint MUST exercise critical dependencies (DB ping, cache ping, downstream sample). Shallow `200 OK` = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Shallow probes route traffic to broken pods.

rule.statement: Production chaos drills run on a documented cadence (monthly tier-0; quarterly tier-1). Untested resilience = theoretical resilience.
rule.rationale: Untested failure modes are unknown failure modes.

rule.statement: DR plan declares RPO + RTO per tier and is drilled at least annually (T0 quarterly).
rule.rationale: Untested DR plans fail in real incidents.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
